title: Bitcoin Clings to Key Support as Weekly US Spot ETF Outflows Hit $1.79B and Fed Rate Hike Bets Mount
summary: Bitcoin is hovering near $60,000 as US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $1.79 billion in outflows last week, the largest weekly withdrawals since their launch. Analysts cite growing fears of a Federal Reserve rate hike and a broader sell-off in the AI sector as key drivers. The $60,000 level is a critical support that, if broken, could lead to further declines.
body:
Bitcoin is hovering just above the $60,000 mark, a crucial support level, as investors react to significant outflows from US spot Bitcoin ETFs. Last week, these funds saw a record $1.79 billion in withdrawals, marking the largest weekly outflows since their launch in 2024. Analysts point to growing concerns about potential Federal Reserve rate hikes and a broader sell-off in the AI sector as key drivers behind the market downturn.

However, the article does not provide any specific commentary on what everyday investors should do or recommendations about diversification, risk management, or buying opportunities. The piece focuses strictly on the market dynamics and analyst views. Bitcoin's price movements are increasingly tied to macroeconomic factors, such as Fed policy and broader market sentiment.

Looking ahead, the key factors to watch are any signals from the Federal Reserve regarding interest rates, as well as developments in the AI sector. If Bitcoin breaks below $60,000, it could trigger further sell-offs, while a rebound above this level could signal a recovery. Staying informed about these factors will be crucial for navigating the current market conditions.
twitterThread: Bitcoin is clinging to $60K as US spot ETFs saw a record $1.79B in weekly outflows — the largest since launch. Fed rate hike fears and AI sector sell-offs are driving the downturn, analysts say.
The outflows come as markets price in a potential Fed rate hike, which could tighten financial conditions and reduce risk appetite. The $60K level is now a key support to watch closely.

If Bitcoin breaks below $60K, further sell-offs could follow. Watch for Fed signals and AI sector developments. Read more here: https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-29-bitcoin-dips-near-60k-as-spot-etfs-see-record-outflows
standaloneTweet: Bitcoin is testing $60K as US spot ETFs see a record $1.79B in weekly outflows. Fed rate hike fears and AI sector sell-offs are key catalysts. This support level could determine the next move.
